name: rust-crate

applies_to.description: Rust library crate with src/lib.rs as the library entry point, tests/ for integration tests, benches/ for benchmarks, and examples/ for usage examples. Managed by Cargo (Cargo.toml at root). Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock are load-bearing at root and must not be moved.

default_rules.note: Rust source files under src/ are at their canonical location; src/lib.rs is the crate root and every file under src/ is part of the module tree
Integration test files under tests/ are at their canonical location; Cargo discovers integration tests here automatically
Benchmark files under benches/ are at their canonical location; Cargo discovers criterion/iai benchmarks here automatically
Example programs under examples/ are at their canonical location; cargo run --example <name> discovers them here

Rust test files not already under tests/ should be relocated there; convention: integration tests (using the public API) live in tests/, while unit tests (using #[cfg(test)] mod) live in src/ inline — ASK_USER if the file appears to be a unit-test module that should stay in src/; hint: integration tests live in tests/
Standard root-level documentation files are canonically at project root; must evaluate before the loose-markdown rule t7 to prevent misrouting
Loose markdown at project root not matched by t6 should move to docs/; if docs/ does not yet exist, TRIAGE should ASK_USER whether to create it or route to an alternative location
Files whose basename begins with 'tmp' or 'scratch' are temporary work artefacts; quarantine to .delete — always ASK_USER before ratifying .delete moves
Backup files (.bak) are editor/tool artefacts that should not accumulate in the crate; quarantine to .delete

Files and directories named old_*, backup/, or v<N>/ are historical snapshots that predate proper version control; ASK_USER — likely .archive but the user decides whether to delete or archive since these may contain unreleased experiment code

notes: Rust library crate template. Cargo.toml is load-bearing at root — ORGANIZE must never propose moving it. Cargo.lock is listed in ignore_paths because ORGANIZE should not classify or move it, but Cargo expects it at root for reproducible builds; its presence/absence is a project decision, not an organization issue. Rule t5 (**/test_*.rs → tests/) must be applied with care: Rust unit tests are typically inline (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) inside src/ files, while integration tests live in tests/ as separate files. A .rs file named test_*.rs that lives outside tests/ is likely an integration test that was misplaced. If TRIAGE is uncertain, emit ASK_USER with the rationale.
